title: Drug Testing

jokes.text: You look cute today, Dwight. / Thanks, girl.
jokes.setup: Jim gives Dwight an unexpectedly feminine compliment
jokes.punchline: Dwight responds with casual 'Thanks, girl' as if this is normal workplace banter
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Dwight's oblivious acceptance of the feminine address is classic. Economy and character integration are excellent.

jokes.text: Jim's list of marijuana symptoms perfectly describing Kevin
jokes.setup: Jim lists symptoms of marijuana use in talking head
jokes.punchline: The symptoms (slow, inattentive, dull, constantly snacking) obviously describe Kevin's normal behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational humor that uses established character traits. The implication is clear and hilarious without being stated.

jokes.text: And by the way, I haven't.
jokes.setup: Dwight claims he didn't become a deputy to make friends
jokes.punchline: Deadpan admission that he indeed hasn't made friends
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - three words that reveal Dwight's social isolation with deadpan honesty. Classic character beat.

jokes.text: I enjoy the tangy zip of Miracle Whip.
jokes.setup: Random confessional moment
jokes.punchline: Dwight's earnest endorsement of Miracle Whip with brand-speak language
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random Dwight moment. The formal phrasing 'tangy zip' makes it funnier than just saying he likes it.

jokes.text: Pam's failed Phyllis impression attempt
jokes.setup: Pam tries to demonstrate Jim's impression skills by doing Phyllis
jokes.punchline: Her attempt is so bad it's just incomprehensible sounds
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor from Pam's earnest failure. Not exceptional but fits her character's awkward earnestness.

jokes.text: Stop! This is not Kelly Kapur story hour.
jokes.setup: Kelly launches into typical rambling personal story during serious investigation
jokes.punchline: Dwight's exasperated interruption with 'story hour' reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect clash between Kelly's narcissistic storytelling and Dwight's intense seriousness. 'Story hour' is a great phrase.

jokes.text: Dude, where's my office? I totally lost it! 'Cause I was half-baked!
jokes.setup: Michael pretending to be high
jokes.punchline: Combines 'Where's My Car?' reference with 'lost it' double meaning and 'half-baked' pun
jokes.explanation: Michael's try-hard attempt at drug humor. Multiple puns but feels forced - exactly right for his character but not great comedy.

jokes.text: I don't think Michael's ever done drugs. I don't know if anyone's ever offered him any.
jokes.setup: Michael's over-the-top fake drug behavior
jokes.punchline: Jim's dry observation about Michael's social isolation
jokes.explanation: Devastating character assassination delivered with Jim's signature dry wit. The second line makes it brutal and specific.

jokes.text: Have you ever... pooped a balloon?
jokes.setup: Dwight interrogating Oscar about potential drug smuggling
jokes.punchline: Awkward pause before the ridiculous 'pooped a balloon' drug mule question
jokes.explanation: Absolutely perfect Dwight moment. The pause builds tension, then the ridiculous specificity of 'pooped a balloon' is comedy gold.

jokes.text: That is Northern Lights Cannabis Indica. / No. It's marijuana.
jokes.setup: Dwight shows off his drug expertise with specific strain knowledge
jokes.punchline: Jim's flat, simple correction deflates Dwight's pretension
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim vs. Dwight dynamic. Dwight's over-specific knowledge met with Jim's dismissive simplicity. Perfect character contrast.

jokes.text: Jinx. Buy me a coke.
jokes.setup: Dwight and Stanley both say 'I do not think that is funny' at the same time
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately calls jinx with childish glee
jokes.explanation: Dwight's childish excitement about jinx rules contrasts perfectly with the serious workplace setting.

jokes.text: Sold out! That has never happened in the history of jinx!
jokes.setup: Jim can't find a Coke in the vending machine
jokes.punchline: Dwight treats this as a historic jinx crisis
jokes.explanation: Dwight's dramatic escalation of jinx importance is perfectly in character. 'History of jinx' is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: Two nights ago I went to an Alicia Keys concert... I think I may have gotten high accidentally by a girl with a lip ring.
jokes.setup: Jim's detailed story about the concert
jokes.punchline: The absurd specificity of getting high 'by a girl with a lip ring' and his naive certainty it was clove cigarettes
jokes.explanation: Jim's uncharacteristic panic leading to over-explanation. The 'lip ring' detail and passive voice 'gotten high by' are funny specifics.

jokes.text: Take Cheech and Chong. Everybody knows that Cheech and Chong are funny. But just imagine how funny they would be if they didn't smoke pot.
jokes.setup: Michael's anti-drug speech
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards logic that drug-based comedians would be funnier without drugs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael logic - confidently stated complete nonsense. The premise is hilariously backwards and delivered with total conviction.

jokes.text: One of those people will be dead from drug use at some point in their lives.
jokes.setup: Michael has everyone look left and right
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous statistic delivered as if it's scientific fact
jokes.explanation: Michael's made-up statistics delivered with D.A.R.E. presentation seriousness. The vagueness of 'at some point' makes it absurd.

jokes.text: Hookah is not an illegal drug, Michael. / Yes, it is. / No, it's not. It's a type of pipe.
jokes.setup: Michael presents his handwritten list of illegal drugs
jokes.punchline: Michael confidently wrong about basic drug knowledge, arguing with HR
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael vs. Toby dynamic. Michael's stubborn ignorance paired with Toby's patient correction creates perfect tension.

jokes.text: Jim's fake crying performance
jokes.setup: Michael pressures Jim to share a tragic drug story about a relative
jokes.punchline: Jim performs elaborate fake crying that everyone buys into
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual comedy moment. Jim's manipulation of Michael's expectations combined with the group's awkward sympathy response.

jokes.text: You might remember testing my urine a few years back when I was applying to be a Volunteer Sheriff's Deputy. / We test a lot of urine. / Mine was green.
jokes.setup: Dwight tries to bond with urine tester over their history
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that his urine was green
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - the matter-of-fact delivery of 'Mine was green' as if this explains everything. Wonderfully weird.

jokes.text: I think I may have gotten high accidentally by a girl with a lip ring.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to explain why he needs clean urine
jokes.punchline: Repeats the same absurd phrasing about the lip ring girl
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier moment. The passive construction 'gotten high by' continues to be funny but loses some impact on repetition.

jokes.text: I want him to have all the urine he needs.
jokes.setup: Jim asks if Dwight wants to give Michael his urine
jokes.punchline: Dwight's earnest, generous response about urine provision
jokes.explanation: Dwight's sincere loyalty expressed through willingness to provide unlimited urine. The earnestness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: His father's name? Dwiged Schrued.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his family naming tradition
jokes.punchline: The Amish version of 'Dwight Schrute' is phonetically mangled
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that feels authentic to Dwight's character. 'Dwiged Schrued' is perfectly mangled Amish pronunciation.

jokes.text: My dad cheated a lot, but I never busted him on it. I would have, except I didn't know about it.
jokes.setup: Jim talking about playing games with his dad
jokes.punchline: The logical contradiction of not busting him because he didn't know
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection joke. The circular logic is mildly amusing but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Jim's prolonged jinx torture of Dwight
jokes.setup: Dwight desperately wants to tell Jim something but can't speak due to jinx rules
jokes.punchline: Jim's sadistic enjoyment of Dwight's frustrated silence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the jinx premise. Jim's cruelty and Dwight's adherence to arbitrary rules create sustained tension.

jokes.text: I took an oath when I was sworn in. And I broke that oath today.
jokes.setup: Dwight resigned from his volunteer deputy position
jokes.punchline: His genuine anguish over breaking an oath for a volunteer position
jokes.explanation: Dwight's sincere moral crisis over a volunteer position shows his character's earnest nature. The gap between importance and reality is funny.

jokes.text: Can I have a gun? / No. I don't have a gun. / I'll have to bring in my bow staff.
jokes.setup: Michael makes Dwight security supervisor
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately asks for weapons, settles on bringing his bow staff
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight escalation - from reasonable (gun) to absurd (bow staff). Shows his character's intensity and medieval interests.

jokes.text: Dwight was literally carrying around his own urine and dressed like one of the Village People.
jokes.setup: Jim reflecting on Dwight's behavior during the episode
jokes.punchline: Summary of Dwight's absurd actions with Village People comparison
jokes.explanation: Good summary joke that highlights the absurdity of Dwight's day. Village People comparison is apt and funny.

jokes.text: Dwight's elaborate search warrant explanation
jokes.setup: Ryan refuses to give Dwight his keys
jokes.punchline: Dwight's overly detailed, bureaucratic explanation of the 'hard way'
jokes.explanation: Dwight's knowledge of proper procedure combined with his volunteer status creates good tension. The detailed explanation builds absurdity.

jokes.text: You're totally harshing the office mellow.
jokes.setup: Ryan trying to defuse Dwight's investigation
jokes.punchline: Using stoner slang 'harshing the mellow' in corporate context
jokes.explanation: Ryan's attempt to be cool with drug slang. Mild humor from the workplace/stoner language clash.

jokes.text: Rattin' somebody out. Narc. Narc!
jokes.setup: Dwight makes a phone call during the investigation
jokes.punchline: Kevin's simple-minded commentary escalating to repeated 'Narc!'
jokes.explanation: Kevin's childlike understanding of the situation. The repeated 'Narc!' shows his limited vocabulary but fits his character.

jokes.text: You wanna work at the urinalysis lab?
jokes.setup: Urine tester mentions they're hiring
jokes.punchline: Michael's immediate interest in a urinalysis job
jokes.explanation: Michael's impulsive career interest fits his character's poor judgment and desperate networking.

tmdb_overview: Dwight plays the role of Volunteer Sheriff after finding half a joint in the Dunder Mifflin parking lot leading to an investigation. Pam gives Jim a play challenge where he must be silent until he gives Pam a can of Coke.
